title: Australia to Launch AU$1.1 Billion Investment in Low-Carbon Liquid Fuels
content: The Australian Government will invest $1.1 billion over 10 years in a Cleaner Fuels Program to accelerate domestic production of low-carbon liquid fuels such as renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel. Aimed at strengthening supply chains and reducing transport emissions, the program will offer production-linked incentives and grants through a competitive process. It builds on existing initiatives like the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Funding Initiative and the Future Made in Australia Innovation Fund. Australiaâ€™s access to renewable energy and abundant feedstocksâ€”including canola, sorghum, sugar and wasteâ€”positions it to develop a thriving low-carbon liquid fuel industry worth an estimated $36 billion by 2050. Local refining would reduce reliance on imports, improve fuel security, and cut emissions from hard-to-abate sectors such as aviation, shipping and construction. The program complements policies such as the Guarantee of Origin Scheme expansion and renewable diesel standards. Government leaders stress the initiative will create regional jobs, back farmers, foresters and innovators, and capture the economic, environmental and supply-chain benefits of the global shift toward net zero.
author: Analysts Account
source: Ministers for Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development and Local Government
